humanSummary.text: Provider ledger hardening passed final blocker audit after reasoning-token and token source contract repairs. One non-blocking benchmark atomicity issue remains recorded as DAG follow-up work.

findings.title: Benchmark artifact recording is not atomic with provider ledger ingestion
findings.description: Audits found benchmark artifacts are linked before provider ledger rows are recorded. If a provider ledger row later fails validation or conflicts, the API can return an error after writing a benchmark artifact without complete ledger state.

evidence.detail: Audit observed recordBenchmarkReport linking the benchmark artifact before iterating provider ledger writes, while the happy-path tests do not cover rollback or partial-ingest behavior.

findings.impact: Benchmark and dashboard reports can observe partial benchmark state, weakening the requirement that reporting reads durable provider ledger state rather than raw prompt logs or report-side cost records.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Benchmark artifact recording and provider ledger ingestion are all-or-nothing for one benchmark report.
Schema-valid skipped provider runs or provider runs with omitted completedAt/latencyMs are accepted when ledger-required fields are otherwise valid.
A regression test proves invalid ledger rows do not leave partial benchmark artifacts behind.

acceptanceCriteria: Benchmark artifact recording and provider ledger ingestion are atomic for success and validation-failure paths.
auditFocus: Partial benchmark artifact writes before ledger validation
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Follow-up node ITOTORI-113 records this non-blocking audit finding.
